free_text: Measurement of 9Be(d,xt) reaction total cross
sections by secondary activation and spectroscopy
methods
English translation
Russian original
EGP-10 tandem accelerator
Entry was approved by Dr. L.N. Generalov.
English
translation. Experiment description is given.
Russian
original.
Self-supporting beryllium target with 658+-33 mu-g/cm2
thick deposited on a thin SiO backing was used.
Six telescopes of dE-E detectors were
used. dE detectors thickness was 12-14 mu-m, E
detectors thickness was 1.2 mm.
Random error consists of random errors in the
parameters that determine the differential cross
sections, errors in specifying angles and implicit
unevenness in target thickness, about 8%.
Systematic uncertainty includes
Error in deuteron beam measurement.
Error in target thickness measurement.
Data were
presented by authors. The same on fig. 6 from
Bull.Rus.Ac.Sci.Phys. 88,1332,2024.
Self-supporting beryllium target with 658+-33 mu-g/cm2
thick deposited on a thin SiO backing was used.
Six telescopes of dE-E detectors were
used. dE detectors thickness was 12-14 mu-m,
E detectors thickness was 1.2 mm.
Total error of the cross sections, about 3%
Data were
presented by authors. The same on fig. 8 from
Bull.Rus.Ac.Sci.Phys. 88,1332,2024.
Differential cross sections are given.
The target was a thick (1 mm) layer of high-purity
beryllium opaque to the beam of deuterons and fast
tritons produced in reaction 9Be(d,xt).
Secondary activation method is used. The fast
tritons produced in 9Be(d,xt) reaction interact with
Be nuclei in the same layer where they originate and
9Be(t,a)8Li reaction was induced.
The target was placed in a cylindrical
scattering vacuum chamber (100 mm in diameter with a
steel wall 1 mm thick) that functioned as a Faraday
cup.
Beta-particles from Li-8 decay were registered.
A plastic scintillator (40 mm in diameter,
h=50 mm) + PMT-93 placed in a lead protective
cylinder (100 mm external diameter, 55 mm internal
diameter and 79 mm high) was used as the
beta-particle detector. The end of the detector's
body was covered with aluminum foil (0.1 mm thick).
The detector was fixed at an angle of 120 degree to
the direction of a beam of accelerated deuterons or
tritons at various distances R from the target. A
window made from lavsan film 0.1 mm thick was in the
chamber's wall in that direction.
Beta-decay curves from Li-8 were analyzed and
absolute yields Ydx8Li and Ytx8Li were determined.
Linear incident triton energy dependence of Li-8
yield was assumed to obtain 9Be(d,xt) reaction total
cross section.
Total error of the cross section.
Error of the beam current measurement
Error of the absolute yield of Li-8 nuclei
from secondary reaction 9Be(t,x)8Li
Error of the absolute yield of Li-8 nuclei
from reaction 9Be(d,x)8Li
Error of the measurement of the ratio of the
efficiency of recording beta-particles from the 8Li
decay produced in deuteron and triton irradiations
